briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to discharge outdated restrictive covenants on private properties
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to discharge (remove) outdated restrictive covenants that the City previously placed on private properties. A restrictive covenant is a legal rule written into a property's title that limits how the land can be used. If approved, this would clear old restrictions that no longer serve a public purpose.
briefs.source_body:
7.1
REQUEST TO DISCHARGE CITY-IMPOSED RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS ON PRIVATE PROPERTIES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the discharge of outdated restrictive covenants imposed by the City on private properties.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to vote on plumbing permit fee changes
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw that would change plumbing permit fees. If approved, the new fees would apply to anyone pulling a plumbing permit in Burnaby. This is not yet law — Council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your permit costs.
briefs.source_body:
7.2
BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - PLUMBING PERMIT FEES AMENDMENT
Purpose:
To propose minor amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

briefs.title: Proposed: Renew Burnaby North Road BIA for 2026-2031
briefs.summary: Council will consider a request to renew the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for another five years, from 2026 to 2031. A BIA is a local group of businesses that work together to improve their commercial area. If approved, local businesses in the area would continue to pay a special levy (a fee) to fund projects like marketing, street cleaning, and events.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your BIA levy.
briefs.source_body:
7.3
BURNABY NORTH ROAD BUSINESS IMPROVEMENT AREA (BIA) RENEWAL
Purpose: To request Council’s approval to proceed with the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association (BIA) request to renew the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area for the period 2026-2031.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to regulate bear spray sales through bylaw changes
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed amendments to the Business Licence Bylaw and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. If approved, these changes would create new rules for selling bear spray in the city. The goal is to reduce misuse and improve public safety. This is a proposal only — it has not been passed yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your retail licence.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
REGULATING THE SALE OF BEAR SPRAY
Purpose: To seek Council approval to bring forward proposed amendments to the Business Licence Bylaw and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to regulate the sale of bear spray in Burnaby.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to consider new hazard rules for Lower Westridge
briefs.summary: Council will consider starting a new Natural Hazard Development Permit Area (DPA) program — a special permit zone for building on risky land — and funding a hazard assessment for the Lower Westridge neighbourhood. This is a proposal, not a final decision.
briefs.source_body: 7.5 NATURAL HAZARD DEVELOPMENT PERMIT AREA AND GUIDELINES AND LOWER WESTRIDGE ASSESSMENT - PROJECT INITIATION Purpose: To seek Council direction regarding the development of a Natural Hazard Development Permit Area (DPA) and Guidelines program for Burnaby and approval of a new budget item to advance a hazard assessment of the Lower Westridge neighbourhood. Natural Hazard Development Permit Area and Guidelines and Lower Westridge Assess

briefs.title: Proposed: Grant right-of-way at 3860 Sperling Avenue for utility works
briefs.summary: Council will consider granting a statutory right-of-way (a legal permission to use a portion of land) over part of 3860 Sperling Avenue (Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre). This would allow the installation, operation, and maintenance of utility works on that land. The decision is not yet final.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
STATUTORY RIGHT-OF-WAY - 3860 SPERLING AVENUE (BURNABY LAKE RECREATION CENTRE)
Purpose: To seek Council approval to grant a Statutory Right-of-Way over a portion of 3860 Sperling Avenue to permit the installation, operations and maintenance of works.

briefs.title: Council to vote on antennae height and coverage variances at 8888 University Drive
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for 8888 University Drive. A DVP is a special permission that allows a property to break certain rules in the zoning bylaw. In this case, the permit would allow existing and future antennae equipment to be taller and cover more of the building's face than normally allowed.
briefs.source_body:
7.7
DVP #25-01 - 8888 UNIVERSITY DRIVE - VARIANCES TO BUILDING FACE COVERAGE AND HEIGHT FOR EXISTING AND FUTURE ANTENNAE EQUIPMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #25-01.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on antenna variances at 9001 University Cres
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for 9001 University Cres. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain city rules. In this case, the owner wants permission to have more antenna equipment on the building face and at a greater height than the current bylaw allows. This would cover both existing and future antennas.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
DVP #25-02 - 9001 UNIVERSITY CRES - VARIANCES TO BUILDING FACE COVERAGE AND HEIGHT FOR EXISTING AND FUTURE ANENNAE EQUIPMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #25-02.

briefs.title: Proposed: Renewal of temporary parking permit at 7000 Lougheed Hwy
briefs.summary: Council will consider renewing a Temporary Use Permit (TUP) for commercial fleet vehicle parking and rentals at 7000 Lougheed Highway. A TUP is a short-term permission that allows a use not normally allowed under current zoning rules. This renewal would let the business continue operating fleet parking on the site for a set period.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your business's parking or rental operations.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
TUP #26-01 (RENEWAL) - 7000 LOUGHEED HIGHWAY
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Temporary Use Permit (TUP) #26-01 renewal for commercial fleet vehicle parking and rentals.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to pay 2026-2027 FCM membership dues
briefs.summary: Council will consider approving payment for the city's 2026-2027 membership in the Federation of Canadian Municipalities (FCM). FCM is a national group that represents cities and towns. If approved, Burnaby would pay its annual dues to stay a member and get access to FCM programs and advocacy.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
2026-2027 FCM MEMBERSHIP DUES
Purpose:
To seek Council approval for payment of the Federation of Canadian Municipalities (FCM) 2026-2027 membership dues.

briefs.title: Council to consider update on proposed Landmark Burnaby Sign
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review an update on the Landmark Burnaby Sign initiative, following previous direction from Council. This is a proposed project that has not yet been approved — Council will consider next steps at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
LANDMARK BURNABY SIGN
Purpose: To provide Council with an update on the Landmark Burnaby Sign initiative following Council’s previous direction.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to authorize special outdoor events for 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to authorize special outdoor events in 2026. This authorization is required under the Burnaby Noise or Sound Abatement Bylaw, which controls noise levels. If approved, the decision would allow event organizers to hold outdoor events at specific locations without breaking noise rules.
briefs.source_body:
7.12
AUTHORIZATION FOR SPECIAL OUTDOOR EVENTS 2026
Purpose: To seek Council authorization, required under the Burnaby Noise or Sound Abatement Bylaw, for outdoor events at specific locations.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on portable toilet contract renewal
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing the contract for portable rental toilets. If approved, the city would continue using the same supplier for portable toilets at parks, construction sites, and special events. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been finalized.
briefs.source_body:
7.13
CR - PORTABLE RENTAL TOILETS
Purpose:
T
o obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Portable Rental Toilets.

briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing security officer services contract
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract for security officer services at various city locations and special events. If approved, the contract renewal would continue security coverage at city facilities and events.
briefs.source_body:
7.14
CR - SECURITY OFFICER SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Security Officer Services at various City locations and Special Events.

briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage Alteration Permit for chimneys at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on issuing a Heritage Alteration Permit (a special permission needed to change a protected historic building) for chimney repairs at the W.J. Mathers House 'Altnadene' on Deer Lake Avenue. The proposed work would alter the chimneys of this heritage property.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
CHC - HAP FOR 6490 DEER LAKE AVENUE
Purpose:
To seek Council approval for the issuance of a Heritage Alteration Permit (HAP) for proposed alterations to the chimneys at the W.J. Mathers House "Altnadene", located at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby on-street parking fee rate increase
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed increase to on-street public parking meter fees in Burnaby. If approved, the new rates would apply to all pay parking spaces. This is not yet in effect — the decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your customers and staff parking costs.
briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - ON-STREET PUBLIC PAY PARKING UPDATE
Purpose: To provide information regarding public pay parking meter spaces and to seek Council approval for the proposed on-street public parking fee rate increase.

briefs.title: Proposed: New energy-based fees for Burnaby's EV charging stations
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposal to change how users pay for public electric vehicle (EV) charging. Instead of a flat fee, the new system would charge based on the amount of energy used (per kilowatt-hour). This change is not yet approved — Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your EV charging costs.
briefs.source_body:
8.3
FMC - PUBLIC ELECTRIC VEHICLE CHARGING NETWORK - ENERGY-BASED USER FEE
Purpose: To propose new energy-based user fees system for City-owned public electric vehicle charging stations.

briefs.title: Proposed: Mixed-use rezoning at 5058 Imperial Street, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to forward a rezoning application (REZ #25-13) for a mixed-use development at 5058 Imperial Street to First and Second Reading. This is an early step in the approval process — if passed, it would allow the project to move forward to a public hearing and final decision. The proposal has not yet been approved.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date to have your say.
briefs.source_body:
8.4
PDC - REZ #25-13 - 5058 IMPERIAL STREET - MIXED USE DEVELOPMENT
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-13 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

briefs.title: Proposed: Forward rezoning for Blake Village at 6800 Lougheed
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to forward a rezoning application (REZ #25-18) for the Blake Village development at 6800 Lougheed Highway to First and Second Reading. This is an early step in the approval process — it does not mean the rezoning is approved yet. A 'reading' is a formal stage where council considers a proposed bylaw; forwarding it means they agree to start the official review.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this could affect nearby property owners.
briefs.source_body:
8.5
PDC - REZ #25-18 - 6800 LOUGHEED HIGHWAY - BLAKE VILLAGE CONCEPTUAL MASTER PLAN UPDATE
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-18 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

briefs.title: Proposed: Mixed-use rezoning at 2209/2225 Springer Ave, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council will consider forwarding a rezoning application (REZ #25-20) for 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue to First and Second Reading. If approved, this would allow a mixed-use development with multiple-family residential units and commercial space on the site. The decision is not final — it is a step in the approval process.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this could affect nearby properties.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
8.6
PDC - REZ #25-20 - 2209 AND 2225 SPRINGER AVENUE - MIXED USE MULTIPLE FAMILY RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT WITH COMMERCIAL
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-20 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby active transportation education initiatives for 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving active transportation education and promotion initiatives for 2026. These programs would encourage walking, cycling, and other non-car travel. A decision has not been made yet — this is a proposed plan up for approval at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
8.7
TC - 2026 ACTIVE TRANSPORTATION EDUCATION AND PROMOTION
Purpose:
To
seek Council approval for active transportation education and promotion initiatives in 2026.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to consider 2026 environmental stewardship events
briefs.summary: Council will receive information about proposed environmental stewardship events for 2026. This is an early look at what events are being planned — no decisions have been made yet. The purpose is to give Council a heads-up so they can consider approving or adjusting the event lineup later.
briefs.source_body:
9.1
EC - 2026 ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP EVENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with information about 2026 environmental stewardship events.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby climate action work plan for 2025-2026
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed work plan for climate action and environment activities in 2025-2026. The plan summarizes what was done in 2025 and suggests new actions for 2026. This is not yet approved — Council will vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
EC - CLIMATE ACTION AND ENVIRONMENT WORK PLAN UPDATE 2025-2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of Climate Action and Environment activities undertaken in 2025 and to propose a workplan for 2026.

briefs.title: Council to consider update on Zero Emissions Building Retrofit Strategy
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive an update on the proposed Zero Emissions Building Retrofit Strategy. This strategy would set new rules for making existing buildings more energy-efficient and lower their pollution. The update covers what has been done so far, but no final decision has been made yet.
briefs.source_body:
9.3
EC - ZERO EMISSIONS BUILDING RETROFIT STRATEGY UPDATE
Purpose: To provide Council with an update on the implementation of the Zero Emissions Building Retrofit Strategy to date.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to refer Stoney Creek construction impacts to staff
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on referring concerns about construction impacts on Stoney Creek to city staff for a report. The proposal asks staff to look into possible ways to reduce harm from construction near the creek. No decision has been made yet — this is a first step to gather more information.
briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - REFER TO STAFF: CONSTRUCTION IMPACTS ON STONEY CREEK - PROPOSED MITIGATION MEASURES
Purpose: To refer the delegation's concerns to staff for report.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Vacancy Management Program 2026 governance framework
briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a proposed Vacancy Management Program for 2026. The program would create a new governance framework and decision-making process to manage vacant properties. Its goals are to improve fiscal stewardship, keep city services sustainable, and align with Council priorities. This is a proposal — it has not been approved yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your property or business.
briefs.source_body:
9.5
FMC - VERBAL REPORT: VACANCY MANAGEMENT PROGRAM 2026 - SUPPORTING RESPONSIBLE GROWTH
Purpose: To provide an overview of the Vacancy Management Program, including the proposed governance framework, decision-making process, and the intended benefits for fiscal stewardship, service sustainability, and alignment with Council priorities.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Football Club request sent to staff for review
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on referring a request from the Burnaby Football Club to city staff for further study. This means the club's proposal will be looked at by staff before any final decision is made. No new rules or fees are being decided yet.
briefs.source_body:
9.6
PRCC - REFER TO STAFF: BURNABY FOOTBALL CLUB DELEGATION
Purpose: To refer the Burnaby Football Club's request to staff.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to complete Social Infrastructure Needs Assessment
briefs.summary: Council will consider a completed Social Infrastructure Needs Assessment and its Summary Report. This study looks at what community facilities and services (like recreation centres, libraries, and childcare spaces) are needed as the city grows. A decision on next steps is expected at the upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
9.7
PDC - OCP RESEARCH - SOCIAL INFRASTRUCTURE NEEDS ASSESSMENT - PROJECT COMPLETION
Purpose: To provide Council with information on the completed Social Infrastructure Needs Assessment and associated Summary Report.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to refer Stoney Creek watershed concerns to staff
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to refer concerns from the Stoney Creek Environment Committee to city staff for a report. The goal is to make sure new development in the Stoney Creek watershed aligns with upcoming planning work. This is not a final decision — it is a step to gather more information before any rules change.
briefs.source_body:
9.8
PDC - REFER TO STAFF: STONEY CREEK ENVIRONMENT COMMITTEE - STONEY CREEK WATERSHED: ALIGNING DEVELOPMENT WITH UPCOMING PLANNING WORK
Purpose: To refer the Stoney Creek Environment Committee's concerns to staff for report.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to review Burnaby Rent Bank statistics 2021–2025
briefs.summary: Council will consider a report on the Burnaby Rent Bank's performance from 2021 to 2025. The Rent Bank provides interest-free loans to help low-income renters avoid eviction or utility shutoffs. This is an information item only — no bylaw or policy change is being voted on at this time.
briefs.source_body:
9.9
PDC - BURNABY RENT BANK STATISTICS
Purpose: To provide Council with information on statistics for the Burnaby Rent Bank between 2021 - 2025.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Planning Committee sets work plan for 2026
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed work plan for the Planning and Development Committee (PDC) for 2026. This plan outlines what development and planning issues the committee will focus on next year. A decision has not been made yet.
briefs.source_body:
9.10
PDC - PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT COMMITTEE WORK PLAN UPDATE 2025 - 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of activities of the Planning and Development Committee (PDC) during 2025 and proposing a work plan for 2026.

briefs.title: Council to review 2024-2025 transportation projects and 2026 plans
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider a report that highlights key transportation projects from 2024-2025 and introduces major initiatives planned for 2026. This is an overview for discussion — no decisions have been made yet on future projects.
briefs.source_body:
9.11
TC - 2024-2025 TRANSPORTATION PROJECT HIGHLIGHTS AND UPCOMING MAJOR INITIATIVES
Purpose:
To provide Council with an overview of key transportation project highlights for 2024-2025 and to introduce major initiatives planned for 2026.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to create five new zoning districts under 2050 plan
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create five new zoning districts and make related changes to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw. This is part of the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) — the city's long-term land use framework. The new districts would change what types of buildings and businesses are allowed in certain areas. The decision has not been made yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date to have your say.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14792 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026 - Text Amendment
Purpose: To propose amendments to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to establish five new zoning districts and related changes to implement the Burnaby 2050 OCP land use framework

briefs.title: Proposed: 8-storey rental building at 7305 & 7317 Buller Ave
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw change that would allow an eight-storey apartment building with 183 market rental units at 7305 and 7317 Buller Avenue. The project includes underground parking. This is not yet approved — Council will consider the rezoning application at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit comments before Council votes.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14794 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 - REZ #25-07 (7305 & 7317 Buller Ave.)
Purpose: to permit the construction of an eight-storey residential apartment building with 183 market rental units above and underground parkade

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area Bylaw 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a new Business Improvement Area (BIA) along Burnaby North Road. A BIA is a special zone where local businesses pay an extra levy to fund joint marketing, beautification, and other projects to improve the area. This bylaw would officially designate the boundaries and rules for the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to have your say.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14797 - Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to designate the area of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw to regulate bear spray sales
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed change to Burnaby's Business Licence Bylaw. If approved, the new rules would add special conditions for businesses that sell bear spray. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your licence.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14798 - Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2025
Purpose: To amend Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017 (bear spray regulations)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw change to enforce bear spray rules
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update how the city enforces rules about bear spray. The amendment to the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw would allow the city to issue tickets and fines for bear spray violations, rather than having to go through a longer court process. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this could affect how bear spray is regulated in your business.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14799 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: To amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (bear spray regulations)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to vote on increasing plumbing fees
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would increase fees for plumbing permits and inspections in Burnaby. The amendment to the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw would raise the cost of plumbing-related services. This is not yet approved — the decision is upcoming.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — higher fees could affect your project costs.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14800 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (plumbing fees)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on advisory body rule changes
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the rules for how advisory bodies (committees that give advice to the city) are set up and run. The amendment would update the 'Terms of Reference' — the official document that describes each committee's purpose, membership, and rules. This is not yet approved.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.5
#14801 - Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: To amend Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 (Terms of Reference for Advisory Body)

briefs.title: Proposed: 204 rental units at 8265-8303 10th Ave up for council vote
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw change that would allow two six-storey apartment buildings with 204 rental units and underground parking at 8265-8303 10th Avenue. The rezoning would create purpose-built rental housing, meaning the units are designed specifically for long-term renters, not condos for sale.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit comments before council votes.
briefs.source_body:
11.3.1
#14780 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 17, 2025 - REZ #25-08 (8265/69/73/77/81/87/89/93 and 8303 10th Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of two six-storey low-rise residential apartment buildings on the subject sites, with a total of 204 purpose-built rental units and underground parking

briefs.title: Proposed: Drug-permitted shelter at 3020 Gilmore faces petition
briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a petition from the WeVote Burnaby Gilmore Shelter Watch group against a proposed drug-permitted shelter at 3020 Gilmore Diversion. The shelter would allow drug use on site, and the petition asks council to reject or change the plan. A final decision has not been made yet.
briefs.action_required: Attend the council meeting or submit your comments before the vote.

key_dates.label: Council meeting to consider petition on proposed shelter

briefs.source_body:
12.1
PETITION: Proposed Drug-Permitted Shelter at 3020 Gilmore Diversion
Purpose: To provide a petition from the WeVote Burnaby Gilmore Shelter Watch regarding the proposed drug-permitted shelter at 3020 Gilmore Diversion, Burnaby.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to address shelter operations at 3020 Gilmore Diversion
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a motion from Councillor Lee to proactively address issues related to shelter services, community integration, and City resource allocation at the 3020 Gilmore Diversion BC Housing project. This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect nearby businesses and properties.
briefs.source_body:
13.1
PROPOSED MOTION: Councillor Lee - 3020 Gilmore Diversion BC Housing Project
Purpose: To seek Council approval to proactively address issues related to service provisions, community integration, and effective allocation of City resources associated with shelter operations at 3020 Gilmore Diversion.
